fingerprint: Sub-terrestrial cavity formula uses δ = 6,371 km — Earth's mean radius — as its scale parameter. Angular shadow zone (104°–140°) is spherical coordinate terminology that has no meaning on a flat disc.
globe_value: Earth's mean radius R = 6,371 km; P-wave shadow zone at 104°–140° derived from Snell's law on concentric spherical shells with core radius 3,480 km
dome_value: Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e^{−r/δ}) where δ = 6,371 km; 'Sub-Terrestrial Wall' claimed to produce shadow zone at 104°–140°

fingerprint_1_delta_equals_R_Earth.description: The dome's sub-terrestrial cavity formula explicitly uses δ = 6,371 km as its 'terrestrial layer scale.' This is the mean radius of the spherical Earth, adopted as a dome parameter with no derivation from dome geometry. The dome's model page provides no explanation for why a flat disc's sub-terrestrial layer should have a scale length equal to the radius of a sphere the dome claims doesn't exist. This joins the disc radius a = 20,015 km = πR_Earth as a second fundamental dome parameter that IS a globe constant.
fingerprint_1_delta_equals_R_Earth.why_it_matters: A flat disc has no intrinsic length scale of 6,371 km. This number arises from the Earth being a sphere of radius R — from which the circumference (2πR = 40,075 km), the semi-circumference (πR = 20,015 km = disc radius), and now the sub-terrestrial scale (R = 6,371 km) are all derived. The dome model is built on top of globe geometry at every level.

fingerprint_2_angular_coordinates.description: The shadow zone is specified at '104°–140° angular distance from the epicenter.' Angular distance is a spherical coordinate: it measures the central angle subtended by two points on a sphere. On a flat disc, distance is measured in km, not degrees. The dome model uses degrees because the underlying seismological data was collected and analyzed using spherical Earth assumptions. Even the concept of 'shadow zone at 104°' presupposes a spherical coordinate system.
fingerprint_2_angular_coordinates.why_it_matters: On a flat disc of radius 20,015 km, there's no natural reason to express seismic distances in degrees. Converting: 104° on a sphere corresponds to 104/360 × 2πR = 11,567 km surface distance. On the dome's disc (azimuthal equidistant projection), this is a radial distance of ~11,567 km from the epicenter. But the shadow zone's angular boundaries are derived from Snell's law on SPHERICAL SHELLS — there's no disc geometry that produces a shadow at this specific radial distance.

fingerprint_3_shadow_encodes_core_ratio.description: The inner shadow boundary at 104° encodes the ratio of core radius to Earth radius. The critical P-wave ray that just grazes the core-mantle boundary (CMB at 2,891 km depth, core radius 3,480 km) produces the shadow edge. This ratio r_core/R_Earth = 3,480/6,371 = 0.546 is a purely spherical geometric quantity. The dome has adopted the observed shadow angles without providing any disc-geometric derivation for why a 'Sub-Terrestrial Wall' would produce shadows at these specific values.
fingerprint_3_shadow_encodes_core_ratio.why_it_matters: The shadow zone boundaries are not just empirical — they were computed FROM spherical geometry by Gutenberg in 1913. The dome can't claim the shadow zone 'matches dome geometry' when the shadow zone's very numbers were derived from globe geometry.

code_search.found_in: model.html: Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e^{−r/δ}) where δ = 6,371 km. No P-wave or seismic content in the dome model page — the 'Sub-Terrestrial Wall' mechanism is only mentioned on the wins page, not developed in the model.
code_search.notes: δ = 6,371 km appears as a stated parameter with no derivation. The dome's model page does not connect the sub-terrestrial formula to seismic observations, P-wave refraction, or any angular shadow zone prediction. WIN-064 on the wins page claims the 'Sub-Terrestrial Wall creates a physical P-wave shadow zone' but the model page's only sub-terrestrial formula describes aetheric flow, not seismic wave propagation. No ray-tracing, no velocity model, no wave equation is provided.

additional_finding_duplicate_paragraph: WIN-064's detail_evidence in wins.json contains a DUPLICATE paragraph. The text 'On a flat disc 20,015 km in radius, there is no spherical liquid core to refract P-waves...' appears TWICE — once mid-text and once at the end. This is likely a merge artifact from a prior patch. Should be fixed.
suggested_text: The dome's sub-terrestrial cavity formula reveals a second globe fingerprint: Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e<sup>−r/δ</sup>) where δ = 6,371 km — the mean radius of the spherical Earth. Just as the disc radius (20,015 km = πR<sub>Earth</sub>) encodes the globe's circumference, the sub-terrestrial scale parameter IS the globe's radius, adopted without derivation. The P-wave shadow zone at 104°–140° is computed from Snell's law applied to concentric spherical shells; these angular boundaries encode the ratio of core radius to Earth radius (3,480/6,371 = 0.546). The dome claims its 'Sub-Terrestrial Wall' creates this shadow, but provides no wave equation, no velocity model, and no ray-tracing showing how a flat boundary produces sharp angular cutoffs that match spherical refraction predictions to better than 1%.
